blocks.content:
Until now, the program has executed code from top to bottom, one line at a time.

But sometimes the same block of code is needed more than once.

For example, you may want to repeat the same action several times:

print messages,
run a loop,
check values.

Copying the same code again and again makes the program longer and harder to read.

A method allows you to move such code into a separate block and give it a name.

Later, the program can run this block by calling its name.

This method contains a loop that prints three levels.

Now the program can run this code by calling the method.
When the program reaches `ShowLevels();`, it executes all the code inside the method.

The method can be called again:

`ShowLevels();`

Each time, the same block of code is executed.
So instead of copying the loop, the program simply calls the method.

Important:

A method does not run automatically.

It runs only when it is called.

A method is a named block of code that can be reused.
